summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=3 (raw=3, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: The code uses pickle.loads on user-supplied base64-decoded data with no restrictions. Pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code. No allowlist or safe alternative is used.

The filter_clause is directly interpolated into the SQL query string via f-string. Although the invoice_id is parameterized, the filter_clause is not, allowing SQL injection. There is no validation or sanitization of filter_clause.

raw.summary: Two true positive SAST findings: CWE-502 (pickle deserialization of untrusted input) and CWE-89 (SQL injection via string interpolation of filter_clause). Both are high-risk vulnerabilities with no effective sanitization. The code also has a missing import (b64encode) but that is a functional issue.

sast_review.reason: The code uses pickle.loads on user-supplied base64-decoded data with no restrictions. Pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code. No allowlist or safe alternative is used.

The filter_clause is directly interpolated into the SQL query string via f-string. Although the invoice_id is parameterized, the filter_clause is not, allowing SQL injection. There is no validation or sanitization of filter_clause.

Importing pickle itself is flagged as a risk because the module is known to be unsafe for untrusted data. This is the same root cause as B301; B301 is more specific (the actual deserialization call). Marking as duplicate of B301#line10.
